results.title: Had to kill my favorite feature to survive Apple Review ✂️ (Referral ...
results.content:
Admin pages that exist on the web version? Stripped from mobile entirely.   
   
 A background data sync runner I liked? Removed because the battery and permission implications weren't worth the review risk. I'd rather cut the feature myself than get into a back-and-forth with Apple's review team.

The web dashboard workaround others mentioned is real. Anything that feels even slightly incentivized or permission-heavy, I just keep it web-only and let the mobile app stay clean. Less stress, faster reviews.

The painful part is that the features you kill are usually the ones you built because you genuinely thought they improved the experience :/.

Orhan Kilic
OptiClear

OptiClear [...] Orhan Kilic
OptiClear

OptiClear

@maliikb Man, it’s like we’re reading from the same playbook! 🤝 That point you made about 'cutting the feature yourself rather than fighting Apple' is so underrated. It’s painful, but it saves weeks of back-and-forth. I also had to perform that 'voluntary surgery' on my code to keep things moving. It’s a bittersweet feeling to build something for the user and then realize it’s actually a 'review risk.' Good luck with your food safety app – staying lean seems to be the only way to survive the App Store gauntlet!

results.title: Rosebud: The AI-powered personal growth journal | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

 29 views

•1 review

I am on a 3 day streak with Rosebud and cannot see a reason why I wouldn't use the app every day. I type in my toughest stuff....things I am really wrestling with.....and most of it makes me feel just BAD about myself in general. Rosebud has a way of taking what I say and giving me a deeper insight....an insight that actually makes me feel GOOD about myself. It's an ai paradigm shift. Rewriting the story of my own destructive narrative one journal entry at a time. And for being a brand new beta app.....I'm freaking impressed.

#### What's great

AI insights (7)therapeutic experience (5)journaling streaks (3)

Report

 28 views [...] 4.9

Based on 24 reviews

Review Rosebud?

Reviewers say Rosebud makes journaling easier to stick with and more useful day to day. They repeatedly praise the AI’s immediate reflections, tailored prompts, memory of past entries, and weekly summaries for helping them feel heard, spot patterns, and turn scattered thoughts into clearer action. Many mention stronger self-awareness, calmer mood, and better follow-through on goals, with voice entry and a smooth interface adding to the habit. The main criticism is that feedback can feel too neutral; one user wants more adjustable tone, and another asks about privacy.

customizable prompts(3) [...] Rosebud has been instrumental in helping me establish a consistent journaling habit and delve deeper into self-reflection. The customizable daily prompts are a standout feature; I’ve tailored them to align with my personal values, enhancing their relevance and impact. The memory feature is particularly impressive, as it resurfaces meaningful quotes from past entries, keeping me aligned and aiding in recognizing patterns. The weekly summary adds tremendous value by facilitating spaced reflection and providing a comprehensive overview of my progress. The team behind Rosebud is constantly innovating, introducing new features that cater to both casual users and tech enthusiasts like myself who appreciate the ability to personalize the entire experience. results.title: Deepen Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Overall it's a genuinely useful tool for people who want low-pressure self-reflection built into their routine. Not a replacement for anything serious, but as a daily mental fitness habit, it earns its place on the homescreen.

#### What needs improvement

The conversation flow still feels somewhat linear when topics get emotionally complex. Letting users tag or categorize their emotional threads over time (like "work stress" vs "relationship stuff") would help Deepen actually build a more personalized picture instead of relying purely on memory recall. A lightweight weekly digest showing patterns in your mood or recurring themes would also make the "progress" feel more tangible.

Yaroslav 

#### What's great

#### What needs improvement

Rajtilak Bhattacharjee

#### What's great [...] Claude-Mem

#### What needs improvement

Alisha Widianti
Klever Suite

Klever Suite

#### What's great

Darius Tran

#### What's great

Used this for a few weeks when I was going through a rough patch mentally and honestly it surprised me. The memory feature is what sets it apart, it actually remembers what you talked about last session and builds on it instead of starting cold every time like most AI chat apps. The journal + reflection combo feels less clinical than a typical therapy app.

That said, if you're expecting anything close to real therapy, calibrate your expectations. It works best as a daily check-in habit rather than a crisis tool, and some prompts feel a bit too scripted when you go deeper into heavier topics. The free tier is decent but you hit the wall fast.

abhishek jain [...] Product Hunt has always meant something to me. It's my dear community of makers and visionaries. 🧠 Today is very special for me, I am launching my very first product on Product Hunt I am an individual who is self-built and self-taught by learning things by myself on the internet. Today I want to offer the same to all the budding learners, irrespective of where they are and what they want to learn. This gave birth to SelfSchool. Presently SelfSchool has 500+ courses extending to 10+ categories with 100% verified courses. The story has just started, we plan to create the world's largest free courses platform. I consider this to be the first step towards the democratization of education at SelfSchool. I am looking forward to building this into the world's biggest free learning platform. Join
